spec_match.rationale: The wizard maps the Chrome 151 macOS 13 minimum into role-specific operational paths for managed fleets, education labs, and consumer web apps.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified audience switching updates path notes, tasks, and message snippets.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The output now distinguishes IT-admin and end-user/consumer action paths instead of collapsing them into one generic list.

edge_cases.rationale: The previous enterprise-versus-consumer branch gap is covered by distinct path notes and snippets.

references.rationale: The added see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5077742779498496.

design_system.rationale: The added path note and links use design-system variables and existing controls.

summary: Re-tested in Chrome Canary through chrome-devtools-mcp at /v151/chrome-will-remove-support-for-macos-12/upgrade-readiness-wizard/: managed output used an Enterprise path with managed-endpoint copy, consumer output used a Consumer app path with detected-user copy, the ChromeStatus link was present, and console/network output was clean.
